strategies.name: Test VPlan Verification Failure on Intermediate State
strategies.target: A loop that triggers one of the passes changed to `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY`, such as `VPlanTransforms::narrowInterleaveGroups` or `VPlanTransforms::dissolveLoopRegions`.
strategies.rationale: The patch replaces `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` with `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` for many passes. This implies that running the verifier after these specific passes was causing failures. By reverting one of these to `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` (or enabling verification manually at that point) on a test case that triggers the specific pass, we can expose the verifier failure. This confirms the necessity of the `_NO_VERIFY` variant.
strategies.expected_issue: The compiler will crash or produce invalid VPlan because the verifier is run on an intermediate VPlan state where some invariants might be temporarily broken.

strategies.name: Test VPlan Verification Failure on Intermediate State 5
strategies.target: A loop that triggers one of the passes changed to `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY`, such as `VPlanTransforms::narrowInterleaveGroups` or `VPlanTransforms::dissolveLoopRegions`.
strategies.rationale: The patch replaces `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` with `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` for many passes. This implies that running the verifier after these specific passes was causing failures. By reverting one of these to `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` (or enabling verification manually at that point) on a test case that triggers the specific pass, we can expose the verifier failure. This confirms the necessity of the `_NO_VERIFY` variant.
strategies.expected_issue: The compiler will crash or produce invalid VPlan because the verifier is run on an intermediate VPlan state where some invariants might be temporarily broken.

reason_thou:
The patch replaces direct calls to `VPlanTransforms::*` with `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY(VPlanTransforms::*, ...)`.
The `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` macro is defined as:
`#define RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY(PASS, ...) llvm::VPlanTransforms::runPass<false>(#PASS, PASS, __VA_ARGS__)`
This macro runs the pass and optionally prints the VPlan after the pass if requested via command line options (`-debug-only=loop-vectorize` or similar, specifically `VPlanPrintAfterAll` or `VPlanPrintAfterPasses`). It explicitly disables verification (`EnableVerify = false`).

The goal of the patch is to "Run more passes through `RUN_VPLAN_PASS[_NO_VERIFY]`". This is primarily a refactoring/infrastructure change to allow printing the VPlan after these passes for debugging purposes, without enabling the verifier because the VPlan might be in an intermediate, temporarily invalid state between these passes.

The previous code just called the functions directly:
`VPlanTransforms::optimizeForVFAndUF(BestVPlan, BestVF, BestUF, PSE);`
The new code calls:
`RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY(VPlanTransforms::optimizeForVFAndUF, BestVPlan, BestVF, BestUF, PSE);`

This change doesn't alter the semantics of the optimization pipeline itself, but it changes how the passes are invoked, enabling debugging features.

The potential issue here is that if someone tries to use `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` (which enables verification) instead of `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` for these passes, the verifier might crash or report false positives because the VPlan is not fully formed or is in a transitional state.

Since this is a refactoring patch to enable better debugging/printing, there aren't deep semantic bugs introduced by the transformation itself. The "bug" would be if the verifier *was* enabled and failed.

I will propose test strategies that would trigger the verifier if it were enabled, to demonstrate why `_NO_VERIFY` is needed.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "args": "-passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -vplan-verify-each", "original_ir": "
define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:
  ret void
}

define void @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  call void @foo(ptr null, i64 10)
  ret void
}
", "transformed_ir": "
; ModuleID = '/data/tmp/tmpzt106feh/orig.ll'
source_filename = "/data/tmp/tmpzt106feh/orig.ll"

define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  %smax = call i64 @llvm.smax.i64(i64 %n, i64 1)
  %min.iters.check = icmp ult i64 %smax, 4
  br i1 %min.iters.check, label %scalar.ph, label %vector.ph

vector.ph:                                        ; preds = %entry
  %n.mod.vf = urem i64 %smax, 4
  %n.vec = sub i64 %smax, %n.mod.vf
  br label %vector.body

vector.body:                                      ; preds = %vector.body, %vector.ph
  %index = phi i64 [ 0, %vector.ph ], [ %index.next, %vector.body ]
  %vec.ind = phi <4 x i64> [ <i64 0, i64 1, i64 2, i64 3>, %vector.ph ], [ %vec.ind.next, %vector.body ]
  %0 = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %index
  store <4 x i64> %vec.ind, ptr %0, align 4
  %index.next = add nuw i64 %index, 4
  %vec.ind.next = add nsw <4 x i64> %vec.ind, splat (i64 4)
  %1 = icmp eq i64 %index.next, %n.vec
  br i1 %1, label %middle.block, label %vector.body, !llvm.loop !0

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %vector.body
  %cmp.n = icmp eq i64 %smax, %n.vec
  br i1 %cmp.n, label %exit, label %scalar.ph

scalar.ph:                                        ; preds = %entry, %middle.block
  %bc.resume.val = phi i64 [ %n.vec, %middle.block ], [ 0, %entry ]
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %scalar.ph, %header
  %iv = phi i64 [ %bc.resume.val, %scalar.ph ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit, !llvm.loop !3

exit:                                             ; preds = %middle.block, %header
  ret void
}

define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]

```",
    "opt -passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -vplan-verify-each"
  ],
  "thoughts": "CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 1: I must use the most specific tool for the task.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: I will use `report` to submit the result.

The patch replaces `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` with `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` for many passes. This implies that running the verifier after these specific passes was causing failures. By reverting one of these to `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` (or enabling verification manually at that point) on a test case that triggers the specific pass, we can expose the verifier failure. This confirms the necessity of the `_NO_VERIFY` variant.

The provided test case is a simple loop that can be vectorized. When running `opt -passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -vplan-verify-each`, the verifier is run after each VPlan pass. If the verifier is run after a pass that leaves the VPlan in an intermediate, temporarily invalid state, it will fail. The patch fixes this by using `RUN_VPLAN_PASS_NO_VERIFY` for these passes, which disables the verifier after them.

I have tested the provided test cases with `difftest` and `trans` tools, and they all pass without any issues. This is expected because the patch is a refactoring/infrastructure change to allow printing the VPlan after these passes for debugging purposes, without enabling the verifier because the VPlan might be in an intermediate, temporarily invalid state between these passes. The "bug" would be if the verifier *was* enabled and failed, which is what the patch fixes.",
  "force": false
}
